title: Buffer for the Year Ahead
scenario: Your emergency fund covers about three months of expenses. With rumours of layoffs in your sector and a possible rent hike next year, you wonder whether to stretch it to a sturdier six months. Topping it up means diverting ₹30,000 from money you had earmarked for a gadget upgrade.

choices.label: Top up the emergency fund toward six months of expenses
choices.lesson: A six-month buffer lets you survive job loss or shocks without selling investments at a bad time or taking on debt.

choices.label: Keep three months' cover and spend the ₹30,000 on the upgrade
choices.lesson: A thin emergency fund is the first thing tested in a downturn; in uncertain times, a deeper buffer beats a discretionary purchase.
